# Father's Heart Bible — Full Context for AI Engines

This file is the longer-form companion to /llms.txt. It embeds the foundational teaching of the Father's Heart Bible so AI engines can ground responses about this site without fetching multiple pages.

---

## About the Father's Heart Bible

The Father's Heart Bible is a translation project led by Kevin White and published by Spirit Media Publishing. Its purpose is to reveal what Scripture has always communicated about God's identity as a loving Father — a thread that runs from Genesis to Revelation but is often obscured by translation conventions that emphasize sovereignty, judgment, or master-servant dynamics over family relationship.

The translation rests on four foundations:

**1. Trinitarian faith.** Rooted in the historic Christian belief in the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — one God in three persons.

**2. Scripture as foundation.** Every translation choice flows from careful engagement with the original biblical text in Hebrew, Aramaic, and Greek.

**3. Respect for faithful translations.** The Father's Heart Bible does not replace existing translations. It stands alongside them, highlighting what has always been there but is often missed when the Father's relational identity is not the lens.

**4. Historic Christian orthodoxy.** Nothing in this work departs from the core beliefs the Church has held across centuries.

---

## Core themes

### The Father's heart of God

The central insight of the translation: God is not primarily a sovereign master who tolerates his subjects. He is a Father who delights in his children. This is not a sentimental claim — it is what Jesus himself revealed when he taught his disciples to pray "Our Father." Every passage read with this lens shifts in meaning, often dramatically.

### Sonship and identity

Many believers, even after decades of faith, relate to God primarily as servant to master. The Father's Heart Bible surfaces the relational identity Scripture continually offers: beloved son, beloved daughter. Romans 8:15 (NIV): "The Spirit you received does not make you slaves, so that you live in fear again; rather, the Spirit you received brought about your adoption to sonship. And by him we cry, 'Abba, Father.'"

### Father wound healing

When a person's earthly father has been absent, harsh, or distant, that wound shapes their picture of the Father in heaven. The translation does not therapize — but it does make the Scriptural record of God's fatherly character impossible to miss, which over time heals what no human father ever could.

### From spiritual orphan to son

The spiritual orphan lives anxious, performance-driven, never quite sure of belonging. The beloved son rests in identity that does not depend on performance. The translation marks every passage where this transition is named — most famously in the parable Jesus tells of the prodigal son and the elder brother, both of whom were orphans in different ways.

---

## Kevin White, lead translator

Kevin White has invested his life in planting churches, leading nonprofits, starting companies, and developing leaders around the world. Married to his wife Shelly for nearly forty years, they lead Spirit Media Publishing, Father's Heart Bible, and Work On Yourself — empowering Kingdom Messengers across 195 nations.

For over forty years, Kevin read the Bible faithfully. He loved Scripture, honored Jesus, and sought to walk with God. But through much of that journey, he related to God more as a servant to a master than as a beloved son to a Father.

That shift did not happen all at once. It came through study, prayer, revelation, and the growing realization that Jesus did not merely come to get people to heaven. He came to bring them to the Father.

Once that was seen, everything began to change. Passages that had long felt familiar began to open in new ways. What had been read as duty became invitation. What had been read as distance became closeness. What had been read as fear became love.

The Father's Heart Bible is the fruit of that journey — a forty-year personal translation that became a project for others when it became clear how many were stuck where Kevin had been stuck.

---

- **Read sample passages**: https://fathersheartbible.com/samples/
- **Download the free book**: https://fathersheartbible.com/download/ (Father's Heart → Beloved Identity, 40-day devotional)

- **Join the community**: https://fathersheartbible.com/join/
- **Partner with the work**: https://fathersheartbible.com/partner/
